# M8 fault-harness manifest. GENERATED by m8gen. Do not edit.
# IDs are per-axis emitted case numbers (== co-sim Result=<ID>).

## D-side axis (m8_dside.S)
1    CAS.L Rm, Rn, @R0    General
2    MOV.B @(disp, Rm), R0 General
3    MOV.B @Rm+, Rn       General
4    MOV.B @Rm, Rn        General
5    MOV.B R0, @(disp, Rn) General
6    MOV.B Rm, @Rn        General
7    MOV.B Rm,@-Rn        General
8    MOV.L @(disp, Rm), Rn General
9    MOV.L @Rm+, Rn       General
10   MOV.L @Rm, Rn        General
11   MOV.L Rm, @(disp, Rn) General
12   MOV.L Rm, @Rn        General
13   MOV.L Rm,@-Rn        General
14   MOV.W @(disp, Rm), R0 General
15   MOV.W @Rm+, Rn       General
16   MOV.W @Rm, Rn        General
17   MOV.W R0, @(disp, Rn) General
18   MOV.W Rm, @Rn        General
19   MOV.W Rm,@-Rn        General
20   STC.L GBR, @-Rn      General
21   STS.L MACH, @-Rn     General
22   STS.L MACL, @-Rn     General
23   STS.L PR, @-Rn       General
24   TAS.B @Rn            General
# 24 emitted; skipped:
#   ADD #imm, Rn         ADD #imm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   ADD Rm, Rn           ADD Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   ADDC Rm, Rn          ADDC Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   ADDV Rm, Rn          ADDV Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   AND #imm, R0         AND #imm, R0 has no D-side memory access
#   AND Rm, Rn           AND Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   AND.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) AND.B #imm, @(R0, GBR): effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   BF /S label          BF /S label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BF label             BF label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BGND                 BGND has no D-side memory access
#   BRA label            BRA label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BRAF Rm              BRAF Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BSR label            BSR label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BSRF Rm              BSRF Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BT /S label          BT /S label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BT label             BT label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   Break                Break has no D-side memory access
#   CLDS CP0_Rm, CP0_COM CLDS CP0_Rm, CP0_COM has no D-side memory access
#   CLDS CPI_Rm, CPI_COM CLDS CPI_Rm, CPI_COM has no D-side memory access
#   CLRMAC               CLRMAC has no D-side memory access
#   CLRT                 CLRT has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /EQ #imm, R0     CMP /EQ #imm, R0 has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /EQ Rm, Rn       CMP /EQ Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /GE Rm, Rn       CMP /GE Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /GT Rm, Rn       CMP /GT Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /HI Rm, Rn       CMP /HI Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /HS Rm, Rn       CMP /HS Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP /STR Rm, Rn      CMP /STR Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP/PL Rn            CMP/PL Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CMP/PZ Rn            CMP/PZ Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CSTS CP0_COM, CP0_Rn CSTS CP0_COM, CP0_Rn has no D-side memory access
#   CSTS CPI_COM, CPI_Rn CSTS CPI_COM, CPI_Rn has no D-side memory access
#   DIV0S Rm, Rn         DIV0S Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   DIV0U                DIV0U has no D-side memory access
#   DIV1 Rm, Rn          DIV1 Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   DMULS.L Rm, Rn       DMULS.L Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   DMULU.L Rm, Rn       DMULU.L Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   DT Rn                DT Rn has no D-side memory access
#   EXTS.B Rm, Rn        EXTS.B Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   EXTS.W Rm, Rn        EXTS.W Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   EXTU.B Rm, Rn        EXTU.B Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   EXTU.W Rm, Rn        EXTU.W Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   Error                Error has no D-side memory access
#   General Illegal      General Illegal has no D-side memory access
#   Interrupt            Interrupt has no D-side memory access
#   JMP @Rm              JMP @Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   JSR @Rm              JSR @Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   LDC Rm, ASIDR        LDC Rm, ASIDR has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, PTEH         LDC Rm, PTEH has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, PTEL         LDC Rm, PTEL has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, Rn_BANK      LDC Rm, Rn_BANK has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, SPC          LDC Rm, SPC has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, SR           LDC Rm, SR has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, SSR          LDC Rm, SSR has no D-side memory access
#   LDC Rm, VBR          LDC Rm, VBR has no D-side memory access
#   LDC, Rm, GBR         LDC, Rm, GBR has no D-side memory access
#   LDC.L @Rm+, GBR      LDC.L @Rm+, GBR: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDC.L @Rm+, SR       LDC.L @Rm+, SR: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDC.L @Rm+, VBR      LDC.L @Rm+, VBR: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDS Rm, CP0_COM      LDS Rm, CP0_COM has no D-side memory access
#   LDS Rm, CPI_COM      LDS Rm, CPI_COM has no D-side memory access
#   LDS Rm, MACH         LDS Rm, MACH has no D-side memory access
#   LDS Rm, MACL         LDS Rm, MACL has no D-side memory access
#   LDS Rm, PR           LDS Rm, PR has no D-side memory access
#   LDS.L @Rm+, MACH     LDS.L @Rm+, MACH: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDS.L @Rm+, MACL     LDS.L @Rm+, MACL: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDS.L @Rm+, PR       LDS.L @Rm+, PR: control-register memory load leaves snapshot register undefined across a cold-TLB fault (co-sim U-write hang, not a decodable Result)
#   LDTLB                LDTLB is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   LDTLB.R              LDTLB.R is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   MAC.L @Rm+, @Rn+     MAC.L @Rm+, @Rn+: effective address not modelled: dual memory-pointer instruction (only one base register is seeded)
#   MAC.W @Rm+, @Rn+     MAC.W @Rm+, @Rn+: effective address not modelled: dual memory-pointer instruction (only one base register is seeded)
#   MOV #imm, Rn         MOV #imm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   MOV Rm, Rn           MOV Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access
#   MOV.B @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.B @(R0, Rm), Rn: effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOV.B @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.B @(disp, GBR), R0: effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.B R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.B R0, @(disp, GBR): effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.B Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.B Rm, @(R0, Rn): effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOV.L @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.L @(R0, Rm), Rn: effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOV.L @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.L @(disp, GBR), R0: effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.L @(disp, PC), Rn MOV.L @(disp, PC), Rn: effective address not modelled: PC-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.L R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.L R0, @(disp, GBR): effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.L Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.L Rm, @(R0, Rn): effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOV.W @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.W @(R0, Rm), Rn: effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOV.W @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.W @(disp, GBR), R0: effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.W @(disp, PC), Rn MOV.W @(disp, PC), Rn: effective address not modelled: PC-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.W R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.W R0, @(disp, GBR): effective address not modelled: GBR-relative base is not seeded by the fixed register choice
#   MOV.W Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.W Rm, @(R0, Rn): effective address not modelled: indexed @(R0,...) implicit R0 collides with the fixed base register (EA off the mapped page)
#   MOVA @(disp, PC), R0 MOVA @(disp, PC), R0 has no D-side memory access
#   MOVT Rn              MOVT Rn has no D-side memory access
#   MUL.L Rm, Rn         MUL.L Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access

#   RTS                  RTS is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   Reset CPU            Reset CPU writes control reg "PC" (mode-unsafe / not representable)
#   SETT                 SETT has no D-side memory access

#   XTRACT Rm, Rn        XTRACT Rm, Rn has no D-side memory access

# 116 emitted; skipped:
#   AND.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) AND.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   BF /S label          BF /S label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BF label             BF label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BRA label            BRA label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BRAF Rm              BRAF Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BSR label            BSR label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BSRF Rm              BSRF Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BT /S label          BT /S label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   BT label             BT label is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   CAS.L Rm, Rn, @R0    CAS.L Rm, Rn, @R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   JMP @Rm              JMP @Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   JSR @Rm              JSR @Rm is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   LDC.L @Rm+, GBR      LDC.L @Rm+, GBR accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDC.L @Rm+, SR       LDC.L @Rm+, SR accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDC.L @Rm+, VBR      LDC.L @Rm+, VBR accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDS.L @Rm+, MACH     LDS.L @Rm+, MACH accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDS.L @Rm+, MACL     LDS.L @Rm+, MACL accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDS.L @Rm+, PR       LDS.L @Rm+, PR accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   LDTLB                LDTLB is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   LDTLB.R              LDTLB.R is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   MAC.L @Rm+, @Rn+     MAC.L @Rm+, @Rn+ accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MAC.W @Rm+, @Rn+     MAC.W @Rm+, @Rn+ accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.B @(R0, Rm), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.B @(disp, GBR), R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B @(disp, Rm), R0 MOV.B @(disp, Rm), R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B @Rm+, Rn       MOV.B @Rm+, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B @Rm, Rn        MOV.B @Rm, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.B R0, @(disp, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B R0, @(disp, Rn) MOV.B R0, @(disp, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.B Rm, @(R0, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B Rm, @Rn        MOV.B Rm, @Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.B Rm,@-Rn        MOV.B Rm,@-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.L @(R0, Rm), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.L @(disp, GBR), R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @(disp, PC), Rn MOV.L @(disp, PC), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @(disp, Rm), Rn MOV.L @(disp, Rm), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @Rm+, Rn       MOV.L @Rm+, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L @Rm, Rn        MOV.L @Rm, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.L R0, @(disp, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.L Rm, @(R0, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L Rm, @(disp, Rn) MOV.L Rm, @(disp, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L Rm, @Rn        MOV.L Rm, @Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.L Rm,@-Rn        MOV.L Rm,@-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @(R0, Rm), Rn  MOV.W @(R0, Rm), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @(disp, GBR), R0 MOV.W @(disp, GBR), R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @(disp, PC), Rn MOV.W @(disp, PC), Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @(disp, Rm), R0 MOV.W @(disp, Rm), R0 accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @Rm+, Rn       MOV.W @Rm+, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W @Rm, Rn        MOV.W @Rm, Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W R0, @(disp, GBR) MOV.W R0, @(disp, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W R0, @(disp, Rn) MOV.W R0, @(disp, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W Rm, @(R0, Rn)  MOV.W Rm, @(R0, Rn) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W Rm, @Rn        MOV.W Rm, @Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   MOV.W Rm,@-Rn        MOV.W Rm,@-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   OR.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) OR.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   RTE                  RTE is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   RTS                  RTS is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   Reset CPU            Reset CPU accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   SLEEP                SLEEP is Bespoke (dedicated guard)
#   STC.L GBR, @-Rn      STC.L GBR, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   STC.L SR, @-Rn       STC.L SR, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   STC.L VBR, @-Rn      STC.L VBR, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   STS.L MACH, @-Rn     STS.L MACH, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   STS.L MACL, @-Rn     STS.L MACL, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   STS.L PR, @-Rn       STS.L PR, @-Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   TAS.B @Rn            TAS.B @Rn accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   TST.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) TST.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
#   XOR.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) XOR.B #imm, @(R0, GBR) accesses memory; I-fetch needs a separate code page (Task 4)
